sourceMovie.title: Cliffhanger
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Cliffhanger for the same reason it broke through in 1993: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Renny Harlin keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Goldfinger
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Cliffhanger clicked for you, Goldfinger (1964) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Triple Frontier
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Cliffhanger clicked for you, Triple Frontier (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Cliffhanger?
faq.answer: Try Goldfinger, Triple Frontier, Plane—each shares DNA with Cliffhanger while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Cliffhanger?
faq.answer: Cliffhanger (1993) is best described as Action, Adventure, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Cliffhanger worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
